name: Improper Fractions and Mixed Numbers
whatIsIt: A PROPER fraction is less than one (3/4). An IMPROPER fraction has a top bigger than the bottom (7/4) — it's more than one whole. A MIXED NUMBER writes the same thing as a whole plus a fraction (1 3/4). They are two names for the same amount: 7/4 = 1 3/4. We can swap between them whenever it's handy.
whyNeeded: Real amounts often pass one whole — 1½ litres of milk, 2¼ pizzas. Being able to switch between improper fractions (easy for calculating) and mixed numbers (easy for reading) is essential for adding, subtracting and measuring.
realLifeUses: 1½ litres of milk or 2¼ cups of flour in a recipe
Measuring 5/2 metres of cloth as 2½ m
Reading a scale that shows more than one whole

vocabulary.meaning: Top smaller than bottom; less than one (3/4).
Top bigger than (or equal to) bottom; one or more (7/4).
A whole number plus a fraction (1 3/4).

story.title: The Pizza Slices
story.text: Sana has 7 quarter-slices of pizza. 'How many whole pizzas is that?' asks Bhaiya. She groups them in fours: 4 quarters make 1 whole, and 3 quarters are left. 'So 7/4 is 1 whole and 3/4 — one and three-quarters!' Bhaiya nods: 'Same slices, two names.'
story.extractedProblem: 7 quarter-slices = 7/4. Write it as a mixed number.
story.answerInStory: 1 3/4 — four quarters make one whole, with three quarters left.

visual.caption: 7/4 = one whole pizza (4/4) plus 3/4 = the mixed number 1 3/4.

teachingGallery.title: Two names, one amount
teachingGallery.note: Group the parts into wholes to change improper → mixed.

standardMethod.summary: Improper → mixed: divide top by bottom (whole = quotient, fraction = remainder/bottom). Mixed → improper: multiply the whole by the bottom, add the top.
steps: Improper to mixed: divide numerator ÷ denominator. The quotient is the whole; the remainder over the denominator is the fraction.
Mixed to improper: (whole × denominator) + numerator, over the same denominator.

commonMistakes.mistake: Changing a mixed number by adding the whole to the numerator only (2 1/3 → 3/3).
commonMistakes.fix: Multiply the whole by the denominator FIRST, then add the numerator: (2×3)+1 = 7/3.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using the remainder as the whole number when changing improper to mixed.
commonMistakes.fix: The QUOTIENT is the whole; the REMAINDER goes on top of the fraction. 7/4 → 1 (quotient) 3/4 (remainder).

medium.q: Write 9/4 as a mixed number's fraction part only — what is left over? (like 1/4)

challenge.q: Ravi changes 2 1/3 to 3/3. What went wrong?
hintLadder: Don't just add the whole to the top.

questions.q: A fraction with the top BIGGER than the bottom is…

teachBackPrompt: Use food to show it: cut two rotis into quarters, take 5 quarter-pieces, and explain to a grown-up that 5/4 is the same as 1 1/4 — then change a mixed number like 2 1/2 back into halves (5/2).

revisionCard.summary: Proper: top < bottom (<1). Improper: top ≥ bottom (≥1). Mixed: whole + fraction. Improper → mixed: divide top by bottom (quotient = whole, remainder over bottom). Mixed → improper: (whole × bottom) + top, over the bottom.

rememberIt.hook: Mixed number = whole + fraction. To improper: whole x bottom + top
rememberIt.unpack: 2 1/3 = (2x3 + 1)/3 = 7/3. Back again: divide - the whole is the quotient, remainder over the bottom.

studentQuestions.q: How do I turn 2 1/3 into an improper fraction?
studentQuestions.a: Whole times bottom plus top, over the bottom: (2x3 + 1)/3 = 7/3.
They are easier to multiply and divide. Convert back to mixed only at the end to read the answer.
